title: alsine caespitosa
intro: Alsine caespitosa, also known as mountain sorrel, is a low-growing, perennial plant that belongs to the saxifragaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Alsine caespitosa include the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as its potential to reduce the risk of kidney stones. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are often made into teas or infusions, and the roots are used to create tonics and decoctions.
